subject: Biology
title: Fruits: Seed Delivery Systems
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Fruits: Seed Delivery Systems.

hook.text: A fruit is not a treat for you—it's a vehicle for seeds. The sweetness is bait.

buildup.text: Animals eat the fruit and scatter the seeds in their droppings, far from the parent plant.

discovery.text: Botanically, tomatoes, peppers, and cucumbers are all fruits—any structure containing seeds.

twist.text: Some fruits explode. The sandbox tree launches seeds at 70 meters per second like tiny grenades.

climax.text: Fruit co-evolution with animals shaped both—sweeter fruits for better dispersal, bigger animals for bigger fruits.

punchline.text: Every fruit is a bribe for seed delivery.

quiz.question: Why do plants produce sweet, colorful fruits?
options: To attract animals that will disperse their seeds
